workflow.trigger: user gives a ticket number — main thread launches the researcher immediately (gh issue view <id>)

steps.action: user reads findings and decides whether/how to proceed — nothing is built before this
creates bug/<ticket-id>/<short-name> or feature/<ticket-id>/<short-name> branch and implements; localizer runs alongside when new user-facing strings appear
user tests manually and relays fixes to the developer; loop with developer until the user says okay
after the user's okay: writes tests covering the change and improves/updates existing tests it touches; loops with developer if a product defect blocks TESTS: GREEN
REVIEW: RED -> developer fixes -> reviewer again; REVIEW: GREEN -> security starts
VERDICT: RED -> developer fixes, then back through reviewer and security; VERDICT: GREEN -> double green
after double green: verifies git identity is the repo owner's (never an AI/Claude identity), commits (short plain message) on the ticket branch — no push yet
runs automatically right after that commit — refreshes docs/codebase-index.md and .harness/project-index/ and commits the sync on the same ticket branch
pushes the ticket branch (both commits in one push) and opens the PR — no AI fingerprints in commit author, message, PR title/body, or comments

project.name: harnextai
project.description: VS Code / Cursor extension (TypeScript): harness for AI coding agents — agents, skills, memory, Doctor, and ticket workflows
